campaigns.title: Help Gopalan of Akshaya Trust feed and care for rescued abandoned elderly parents
campaigns.description: “Parvathi Amma (name changed) was brought to Akshaya Trust by her son. He brought her here saying she was being taken to the hospital for treatment and he would be back in a while. It's been 8 years since that day. The phone number he gave to us was made up, even his address was false. Imagine the pain of the mother who was abandoned in such a heartless manner. She is not the only one. We have over 260 elderly parents here and most of them have faced abandonment, neglect or even abuse”, says G Gopalan, the founder of Akshaya Trust, the NGO taking care of abandoned seniors who have no one else in the world.Without Gopalan’s Akshaya Trust, countless elderly would still be on the streets or simply deprived of the love and care they deserve. The NGO takes care of every single need of its seniors from Food to medical care and housing. Your donation can enable Akshaya Trust to reach out to many more elderly with food and support. Contribute generously. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).G Gopalan, a well-to-do businessman preferred to serve the elderly than living a peaceful retired life. He says that he was too shocked to see the state of the elderly, treated shabbily by their own children.A lifeline for hundreds of neglected elderlyToday, Akshaya Trust offers a safe haven to over 260 elderly parents, shielding them from the harsh realities of street life. This organization attends to all their needs, providing comprehensive medical care and ensuring they recover from past traumas. The trust also organises joyful celebrations during festivals and special occasions, bringing happiness into their lives.To date, Akshaya Trust has served over 1,000 elderly parents most of whom were from extremely weak financial backgrounds and had no way to fend for themselves. Akshaya Trust: A silver lining for elderlyAkshaya Trust’s efforts are a lifeline for these seniors, but they rely heavily on public support to continue. You can be the hero these elderly parents deserve. Donate now to help care for them.Your contribution can transform the lives of many homeless seniors. Support Akshaya Trust’s mission today.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help elderly parents abandoned by their children get the love and care they deserve.

messages.en:
Gopalan has dedicated his life to rescuing abandoned elderly parents - giving them food, shelter and dignity when their own families left them helpless.

Only 2 days left to raise ₹1.8 crore more for Akshaya Trust's vital work (₹1.2 crore raised of ₹3 crore goal).

Even ₹100 helps save lives. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: 400 elderly parents abandoned by their families need your urgent support. Donate Now
campaigns.description: Vijaya was around 70 years old and a street in Avadi, Chennai was her home. Abandoned by family, she had no roof to call home and no means of survival. The police had warned her to move on. Residents had complained. Nobody wanted to touch what they saw as somebody else's problem. Then someone called Anbagam. The rescue team from the NGO arrived. There was a complication: Vijaya refused to leave without her puppies. The team found homes for each one. Then they brought Vijaya in. Today she is healthy, comfortable, and happy. This is what Anbagam does. It meets people exactly where they are, asks nothing of them except to come, and gives them something most of us take entirely for granted: a safe place to be.But without your support this safe haven for the elderly might not even exist. Be the pillar of support this old age home needs. Donate generously!Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A life-long missionSince 1999, Mohammed Rafi, a small businessman from Parry's in Chennai, has been doing this work. He started by housing people in his own home. When that filled up, he built more. Today, Anbagam runs three homes across Chennai and Thiruvallur, caring for 400 residents: mentally ill, elderly, abandoned, forgotten.Running this for 400 people costs money. Food alone costs roughly Rs 4 lakh every month. Medicines and psychiatric care cost approximately Rs 1 lakh a month. Add staff, a doctor, cleaning, bedding, and clothing, and the annual operating cost across all three homes comes to around Rs 77 lakh.Your contribution means a warm meal, a comfortable bed and necessary medical treatment for an abandoned elderly grandparent. Support Now!Impact beyond numbersOver 7,000 people have walked through Anbagam's doors. Around 3,000 have been reunited with families across India. The rest stay, because Anbagam is the family they have now.Mohammed Rafi said it simply: “Share your happiness with the people who are here. Help them!”Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Anbagam, in its mission to give abandoned elderly parents the care they deserve.

messages.en:
400 elderly parents have been abandoned by their own children and are struggling to survive without basic care and support. These seniors who once took care of their families now have nowhere to turn.

Only 2 days left to raise ₹47.9 lakh more (₹2.1 lakh raised so far). Even ₹100 helps give them dignity and care.

Please donate OR share with 5 people

campaigns.title: Help Dr. Bhagat care for 500+ elderly parents rescued from abuse and homelessness
campaigns.description: For a mother who has spent her entire life loving and raising her child, being beaten and mercilessly thrown out onto the streets by her own son is too heartbreaking to overcome. But this is a common story among the elderly residents of the Guru Vishram Vridh Ashram. “I’ve seen mothers in old and torn clothes, their bodies smelling and their hands and feet rotting and covered in maggots,” shares Dr. G.P. Bhagat, founder of the ashram. These abandoned elderly men and women lay starving on the streets hoping for someone to rescue them from their suffering. But most people walk past them without a second glance. “Like this”, Dr. Bhagat explains, “many elders die on the roads.”Since 2003, SHEOWS (Saint Hardyal Educational and Orphans Welfare Society) has rescued over 10,000 elderly people from the streets and given them a home with care and support. The elderly staying at the ashram have all been abandoned by their families and have nowhere else to live. Your support can give them comfort after their abandonment. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India). Safe and loving homeFor 20 years, SHEOWS has been providing free shelter, nutritious food three times a day, clothing, medical care, recreation activities and emotional support to abused and disabled elderly people rescued from the streets of Delhi. The Guru Vishram Vridh Ashram has a dedicated rescue team that saves the elderly from the streets and gives them immediate medical care upon their arrival at the home. To tend to the multiple health complications of the seniors, the home has a medical facility with doctors and nurses, a physiotherapy unit, a pathology lab, a digital X-ray monitoring machine and a pharmacy to provide 24-hour care to older adults anytime they require assistance, even during emergencies. Dr. Bhagat prioritizes the health of the seniors and ensures that they receive three balanced meals a day based on their precise health requirements. Need for the fundraiserThe humanitarian mission of SHEOWS is making an impact in the area, as more and more people are being rescued and brought to Guru Vishram Vridh Ashram. To meet the financial costs of running the home, SHEOWS needs support to continue providing the residents with all the facilities that they need. This includes constant care and round-the-clock medical attention, nutritious food and maintaining a hygienic environment to keep the seniors in comfort and good health. Dr. Bhagat explains that as the number of residents is increasing, SHEOWS needs support for food and medicines. “I request you to help these elderly parents lead a peaceful life.”Donate now to help Dr. Bhagat continue caring for the 500 destitute elderly and giving them shelter, food, medical care and the will to carry on living despite their tragic abandonment by their families. Your support will enable SHEOWS to keep serving abandoned senior citizens and help them recover from the trauma of abandonment and life on the streets. “I have been serving these elderly as if they were my own parents”, shares Dr. Bhagat. “I want only one thing - their tears should vanish and they should have a smile on their faces.” To help Dr. Bhagat achieve this dream, your support is critical. Donate now to help SHEOWS care for more abandoned elderly. Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support SHEOWS in their mission to rescue the abandoned elderly from the streets and give them shelter, food, medical care, recreational activities and emotional support.

messages.en:
Dr. Bhagat has rescued 500+ elderly parents from abuse and homelessness, giving them shelter and care when their families abandoned them. These grandparents deserve dignity in their final years. Only 4 days left to raise remaining ₹58 lakh of ₹2 crore goal. Even ₹100 helps save a life. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: Help Dr Amruta provide life-saving cancer treatment to poor patients who can’t afford care
campaigns.description: Lakhs of cancer patients across the country are fighting against the dreaded disease with little support or help. Often treatment is costly which poor families can’t afford, leaving patients in a painful battle that they are bound to lose without medical care. Many families even sell their land and property to afford treatment, and still, end up losing their loved ones. “When such cancer patients come to us we stand by them. We hold their hands and assure them that they will get the treatment they need”, says Dr Amruta Tripathi, a compassionate oncologist at Charutar Arogya Mandal.No mother should have to feel the pain of losing a child, no wife of losing her husband or any family member just because they’re poorDr. Amruta has made it her life’s mission to stand by those battling cancer alone, patients rejected by hospitals, forgotten by society. At Charutar Arogya Mandal, she and her team provide free, world-class cancer treatment to the poorest of the poor. But she cannot do it without you. Support her. Donate to ensure no one fights cancer aloneDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A growing crisis that goes unnoticedEvery year, tens of thousands of Indians are diagnosed with cancer and many of them cannot afford even the first consultation, let alone treatment. They are left to suffer till their last breath, often in isolation and agony.“These patients are in unimaginable pain. When they are refused admission everywhere else, they come to us. And I have resolved that I will not leave such patients. I will be there to hold their hand”, Dr Amruta says The moment cancer is diagnosed not just the patients but the entire family suffers. The cost of treatment is exorbitant and they can't afford it. But our doctors stand by these patients, says Dr Atul Haribhai Patel, Chairman of Charutar Arogya Mandal.At Charutar Arogya Mandal Dr Amruta and her team have treated over 30,000 patients free of cost since 2004, offering surgery, radiation, chemotherapy, and even nutrition and post-treatment support, all as a nonprofit whose only objective is to support those suffering.Healing when all hope is lost“The biggest happiness for me is when I see a patient cancer-free. It means our treatment worked. That we gave someone their life back,” Dr. Amruta says, smiling.Each life saved is a miracle — a child who gets to grow up, a mother who gets to see her daughter graduate, a father who returns home to his family. But behind every miracle is a mountain of effort and expense.And now, the centre urgently needs equipment and resources to continue providing this critical care to patients from the most marginalised backgrounds.Your donation can save a lifeWith your support, Dr. Amruta can continue to provide free, compassionate, and quality treatment to cancer patients who have nowhere else to go.Your donation will help the centreProcure life-saving medical equipmentOffer advanced diagnostics and treatmentProvide holistic care from medicine to mealsEnsure no one is turned away for lack of fundsDonate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Charutar Arogya Mandal in its mission to provide support poor cancer patients who can not afford treatment.

messages.en:
Dr Amruta has dedicated her life to treating cancer patients who can't afford care. She's helping families who would otherwise lose hope.

Only 4 days left to raise ₹25.9 lakh more (₹24.1 lakh already raised of ₹50 lakh goal).

Even ₹100 helps save a life. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: Help visually impaired Shivaprakash give 40 blind girls shelter, education and care
campaigns.description: At the age of 8, Shivaprakash lost his vision. But what could have become a life of despair became something else entirely. Shivaprakash went on to complete his Master’s degree and eventually founded the Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust, an organisation born out of personal struggle and driven by a powerful mission, to educate and empower blind girls, who are among the most neglected and marginalised in society.In many families, blind daughters are hidden away, seen as a burden, and in some cases, even as a curse. They are denied the right to dream, grow, or be independent. At Divya Jyothi, Shivaprakash is changing that narrative. Your support can ensure that no blind girl is left behind simply because of her disability. Donate today to help them stand tall, proud, and self-reliant. Donate nowDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Education is survivalIn communities where gender and disability intersect, blind girls face deep-rooted prejudice and extreme isolation. Many are denied even basic schooling and are left dependent on family members for the rest of their lives. Recognising this, Divya Jyothi began with a simple yet radical step: to provide blind girls with computer training so they could access the digital world and open doors to employment.But the journey didn’t stop there. The NGO went on to launch a braille transcription centre, ensuring girls have access to learning materials that suit their needs. As Shivaprakash says, “Without education, no one can do anything. That's why education is even more important for blind girls.” Education isn’t just about literacy, it’s about self-worth, decision-making, mobility, and freedom. It’s about giving these girls the same right to participate in society that sighted individuals often take for granted.From rejection to respectIn a country where blind girls are often treated as liabilities, Divya Jyothi is rewriting their futures. Over 1,000 girls have graduated from the Trust’s training centres. Many of them come from families that had almost given up on them. Today, these same girls are government employees, corporate professionals, and role models in their communities.More than 200 graduates have secured jobs in the public sector. Over 300 are now employed in private companies. Every success story is proof that with the right support, blind girls don’t just survive, they thrive. “Most girls are neglected by their families. This is a major challenge for them,” says Shivaprakash. By providing them with skills and confidence, Divya Jyothi is turning neglect into empowerment and helplessness into hope.Donate to help 40 blind girls find independenceAlmost 40 girls are currently enrolled and will directly benefit from your support. These are girls with potential, courage, and ambition—but they lack the resources to pursue an independent life. “I request each one to come forward and donate to help blind girls lead independent lives in society,” urges Shivaprakash.Help Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust break the cycle of neglect. Your donation can give a blind girl her first lesson, her first job, and a life of dignity.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust in its mission to shelter and educate neglected and abandoned blind girls.

messages.hi:
शिवप्रकाश खुद नेत्रहीन हैं लेकिन 40 अंधी लड़कियों के लिए आश्रम चलाते हैं, उन्हें शिक्षा और आशा देते हैं। उनके आश्रम को चलाने के लिए तुरंत फंड चाहिए।

सिर्फ 4 दिन बचे हैं ₹24.1 लाख और जुटाने के लिए (₹50 लाख के लक्ष्य में से ₹25.9 लाख जुटा)।

सिर्फ ₹100 भी इन बच्चियों की मदद करेगा।
कृपया दान करें या 5 लोगों के साथ शेयर करें
https://give.do/fundraisers/help-visually-impaired-shivaprakash-give-40-blind-girls-shelter-education-and-care
messages.kn:
ಶಿವಪ್ರಕಾಶ್ ಸ್ವತಃ ದೃಷ್ಟಿಹೀನರಾಗಿದ್ದರೂ 40 ಕುರುಡು ಹುಡುಗಿಯರಿಗೆ ಆಶ್ರಯ ನೀಡುತ್ತಾರೆ, ಅವರಿಗೆ ಶಿಕ್ಷಣ ಮತ್ತು ಭವಿಷ್ಯದ ಭರವಸೆ ಕೊಡುತ್ತಾರೆ। ಆಶ್ರಯ ಮುಂದುವರಿಸಲು ತುರ್ತಾಗಿ ಹಣ ಬೇಕು।

₹24.1 ಲಕ್ಷ ಇನ್ನೂ ಸಂಗ್ರಹಿಸಲು ಕೇವಲ 4 ದಿನ ಉಳಿದಿದೆ (₹50 ಲಕ್ಷ ಗುರಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ₹25.9 ಲಕ್ಷ ಸಂಗ್ರಹ)।

₹100 ಕೂಡ ಈ ಹುಡುಗಿಯರಿಗೆ ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡುತ್ತದೆ।
ದಯವಿಟ್ಟು ದಾನ ಮಾಡಿ ಅಥವಾ 5 ಜನರೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹಂಚಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ
https://give.do/fundraisers/help-visually-impaired-shivaprakash-give-40-blind-girls-shelter-education-and-care
messages.ta:
சிவப்பிரகாஷ் தானே பார்வையற்றவர் ஆனால் 40 பார்வையற்ற பெண்களுக்கு இல்லம் நடத்துகிறார், அவர்களுக்கு கல்வியும் நம்பிக்கையும் தருகிறார். அவரின் இல்லத்தை தொடர்ந்து நடத்த உடனடி நிதி தேவை।

₹24.1 லட்சம் இன்னும் திரட்ட வெறும் 4 நாட்கள் மட்டுமே உள்ளன (₹50 லட்சம் இலக்கில் ₹25.9 லட்சம் திரட்டப்பட்டுள்ளது)।

₹100 கூட இந்த பெண்களுக்கு உதவும்।
தயவுசெய்து நன்கொடை அளியுங்கள் அல்லது 5 பேருடன் பகிருங்கள்

campaigns.title: Help visually impaired ‘Singh Sir’ provide food, shelter and education to blind girls
campaigns.description: In many families, blind girls are often seen as a burden—a harsh reality that leaves many of them abandoned, neglected, and deprived of opportunities. Families often feel, "If we spend money on them, it would be useless", shares Ramesh Singh a blind teacher committed to providing care and education the blind girls deserve. Sadly, many blind girls are forced to grow up in isolation and denied the education and care they deserve. But amidst this darkness, there is a glimmer of hope. “Singh Sir” as he is fondly called, is on a mission but he cannot do it alone. Support this blind teacher in transforming lives. Donate generouslyDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).How it beganIn 2005, Ramesh Prasad Singh, a blind teacher who has personally experienced the struggles faced by blind children, founded the Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad. His mission? To rescue blind girls from neglect and give them a chance to thrive."People think this girl is a lifelong burden for us," Ramesh says. "But as soon as the girl gets a job, the same family starts hounding her."Over the years, the nonprofit has provided food, shelter, and admission fees for college to blind girls, ensuring they are not trapped within four walls but instead empowered to pursue their dreams.At the heart of Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad is a home—a safe haven for blind girls where they receive education, care, and the tools to build a better future. These girls are not just surviving; they are thriving. They are becoming independent, proving that blindness is not a limitation but a stepping stone to resilience and strength.But Ramesh cannot continue this mission alone.He appeals to you to join hands and support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad.Why your contribution countsNutritious meals for blind girlsSafe and secure shelterAdmission fees and educational support"We appeal to each person to support our mission and make a difference," Ramesh urges.Together, we can ensure these girls are no longer seen as burdens but as empowered individuals ready to lead fulfilling lives.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad in its mission to provide blind girls the shelter, care and education they deserve.

messages.en:
Singh Sir is a visually impaired teacher who has dedicated his life to educating blind girls, providing them food and shelter when their families can't. He's been running this center with his own savings for years.

Only 4 days left to help him continue this beautiful work. Still need ₹11.7 lakh of ₹50 lakh goal.

Even ₹100 helps. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: Help Vinay bring children facing abuse in red-light areas to safety and protect them
campaigns.description: “I saw a girl, barely 7 or 8, being pushed into flesh trade. Her cries for help still echo in my ears. There were men as old as 60 r*ping the innocent child. That was the day I knew my mission in life was to get these children out of the hell-like conditions in red-light areas. They are nothing short of hell and no child deserves to grow up in this environment” says Vinay Vasta, founder of Social Activities Integration, an organisation committed to freeing children trapped in red-light areas.Vinay and his NGO have been able to provide education, medical care and nutrition to over 2,500 children. But his dream to provide safe shelter to children remains unrealized. “We do our best to keep these children safe during the day but sadly they are forced to go back to the red light areas at night as we don’t have a home for them. Your donation can help us build a safe home for children of sex workers and prevent them from being pulled into prostitution. Donate to make this dream a realityDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Life in red light areas“S*x workers use their homes which are barely 6 feet by 4 feet for their business. They invite customers in the presence of their children. Their children not only witness abuse but experience it too. Just imagine a child being told that all she can ever become in life is a pr*stitute”, Vinay shares as he explains the extent of abuse children face in red light areas.This is a life that thousands of children are forced to live behind closed doors where they suffer abuse and exploitation morning, noon and night.A ray of hopeVinay has been relentlessly trying to give children the care and education they deserve. He has provided quality English education to thousands of children, enabling them to secure a livelihood out of red-light areas. Several children taught by Vinay are today leading successful lives as professionals and Government employees. However the sad reality is that Vinay and his NGO, in the absence of a permanent shelter home, are unable to provide round the clock safety to these children, which is essential for their well-being.Your donations can enable SAI to build a shelter home for the children of sex workers and usher in a new tomorrow for them, away from the horrors of red-light areas.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to provide safe shelter for children of sex workers and save them from abuse and second generation pr*stitution.NGO's 80G number: AACTS0408EF20216NGO's 12A number: AACTS0408EE20028

messages.en:
Vinay has dedicated his life to rescuing children from abuse in red-light areas. His work saves innocent lives from unimaginable trauma.

Just 5 days left to support his mission. The goal of ₹90 lakh is nearly reached - only small amount still needed.

Even ₹100 helps protect a child. Please donate OR share with 5 people.

campaigns.title: Help 72-year-old Vanitha provide 100+ abandoned mentally ill women with shelter and care
campaigns.description: “The major problem in society today is the stigma against mentally ill or mentally disabled people. Families feed and care for men who are mentally ill and keep them at home. But no one wants to help women with mental illnesses,” says N Vanitha Rengaraj, chairperson of Sharanalayam.Sharanalaym’s ‘Jothi’ is a sanctuary for over 100 women facing mental challenges. Women with mental illness facing various challenges on the streets are brought to Sharanalayam with the help of the authorities and cared for. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Donations to this fundraiser are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).The women at Sharanalaym Jothi are provided with necessities like food, clothing and medical care. They also get mental health services, access to psychiatrists and counseling, empowering individuals on their path to recovery.Once lost and vulnerable on unfamiliar streets, the women at Sharanalaym Jothi find solace.By supporting mentally disabled women at Sharanalaym Jothi, you contribute to creating a better world. We can ensure these women receive the care and support they deserve.Over the years, Vanitha has tried her best to run Sharanalayam from her own resources and that of her well-wishers, but the last few years have been harsh. “I normally don’t extend my hand for people to donate. Many have come on their own. But life has become a bit difficult now. I need your support to take care of the women here and also bring more women here in need of help,” says Vanitha. How Sharanalayam came into beingThe NGO started its operations in 2001 from rented premises in Pollachi in the district of Coimbatore. It started with taking care of children in difficult conditions. It slowly started taking care of mentally challenged women. Sharanalayam has come a long way since that time, with hundreds of children and women being taken care of by the NGO in the last two decades. Many women were rehabilitated by the NGO, thanks to its facilities.Sharanalayam needs urgent support to take care of over 100 women. The organisation also aspires to take care of many more mentally ill women in the coming months and years. Vanitha cannot do it without your support. Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help abandoned mentally ill women get care and shelter. Vanitha, 72, the founder of Sharanalayam cannot do it alone.Donate now.

messages.en:
72-year-old Vanitha has dedicated her life to caring for 100+ abandoned mentally ill women who have nowhere else to go. She provides them shelter, food, and the dignity they deserve.

With just 7 days left, we still need ₹1.8 crore of our ₹3 crore goal to secure their future care. Even ₹100 helps save these vulnerable lives.

Please donate OR share with 5 people - every action counts.

messages.en:
A father's world collapsed when his son Subramanian Ganesan suffered a brain stroke. This young man who had his whole life ahead of him now fights for recovery in the hospital.

With only 16 days left, they still need ₹20.4 lakh for his treatment. The family has barely managed ₹13K so far.

Even ₹100 helps save a life. Please donate or share with 5 people.

messages.mr:
मुलगा सुब्रमण्यन गणेशन याला ब्रेन स्ट्रोक आल्यावर एका वडिलांचे जग कोसळले. ज्या तरुणाचे संपूर्ण आयुष्य पुढे होते, तो आता रुग्णालयात बरे होण्यासाठी लढत आहे।

फक्त 16 दिवस शिल्लक असताना, अजूनही ₹20.4 लाख लागतात. कुटुंबाने आतापर्यंत फक्त ₹13K गोळा केले आहे।

फक्त ₹100 सुद्धा एक जीव वाचवू शकतात. कृपया देणगी द्या किंवा 5 जणांसोबत शेअर करा.

messages.bn:
ছেলে সুব্রমণ্যন গণেশনের ব্রেইন স্ট্রোক হলে একজন বাবার পৃথিবী ভেঙে পড়ে। যে যুবকের সামনে পুরো জীবন ছিল, সে এখন হাসপাতালে সুস্থ হওয়ার জন্য লড়ছে।

মাত্র ১৬ দিন বাকি থাকতে এখনও ₹২০.৪ লাখ প্রয়োজন। পরিবার এ পর্যন্ত মাত্র ₹১ৃK সংগ্রহ করতে পেরেছে।

মাত্র ₹১০০ও একটি প্রাণ বাঁচাতে সাহায্য করে। দয়া করে দান করুন বা ৫ জনের সাথে শেয়ার করুন।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1
messages.gu:
દીકરા સુબ્રમણ્યન ગણેશનને બ્રેઇન સ્ટ્રોક આવ્યો ત્યારે એક પિતાનું જગત તૂટી પડ્યું. આ યુવાનનું આખું જીવન આગળ હતું, હવે તે હોસ્પિટલમાં સાજા થવા માટે લડી રહ્યો છે।

માત્ર 16 દિવસ બાકી રહેતાં, હજુ પણ ₹20.4 લાખની જરૂર છે. પરિવારે અત્યાર સુધી માત્ર ₹13K જ એકત્ર કર્યા છે।

માત્ર ₹100 પણ એક જીવ બચાવવામાં મદદ કરે છે. કૃપા કરીને દાન કરો અથવા 5 લોકો સાથે શેર કરો.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1
messages.pa:
ਪੁੱਤਰ ਸੁਬ੍ਰਮਣਿਅਨ ਗਣੇਸ਼ਨ ਨੂੰ ਬ੍ਰੇਨ ਸਟ੍ਰੋਕ ਆਇਆ ਤਾਂ ਇੱਕ ਪਿਤਾ ਦੀ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਢਹਿ ਗਈ। ਇਹ ਨੌਜਵਾਨ ਜਿਸਦੀ ਸਾਰੀ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਅੱਗੇ ਸੀ, ਹੁਣ ਹਸਪਤਾਲ ਵਿੱਚ ਠੀਕ ਹੋਣ ਲਈ ਲੜ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ।

ਸਿਰਫ 16 ਦਿਨ ਬਾਕੀ ਹਨ ਅਤੇ ਅਜੇ ਵੀ ₹20.4 ਲੱਖ ਚਾਹੀਦੇ ਹਨ। ਪਰਿਵਾਰ ਹੁਣ ਤੱਕ ਸਿਰਫ ₹13K ਇਕੱਠੇ ਕਰ ਸਕਿਆ ਹੈ।

ਸਿਰਫ ₹100 ਵੀ ਇੱਕ ਜਾਨ ਬਚਾਉਣ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਦਦ ਕਰਦੇ ਹਨ। ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਦਾਨ ਦਿਓ ਜਾਂ 5 ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਸਾਂਝਾ ਕਰੋ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1
messages.ml:
മകൻ സുബ്രഹ്മണ്യൻ ഗണേശന് ബ്രെയിൻ സ്ട്രോക്ക് വന്നപ്പോൾ ഒരു പിതാവിന്റെ ലോകം തകർന്നു. മുഴുവൻ ജീവിതവും മുന്നിൽ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്ന ഈ യുവാവ് ഇപ്പോൾ ആശുപത്രിയിൽ സുഖപ്പെടാൻ പോരാടുകയാണ്।

16 ദിവസം മാത്രം ബാക്കി നിൽക്കെ, ഇനിയും ₹20.4 ലക്ഷം വേണം. കുടുംബത്തിന് ഇതുവരെ വെറും ₹13K മാത്രമേ ശേഖരിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞിട്ടുള്ളൂ।

വെറും ₹100 പോലും ഒരു ജീവൻ രക്ഷിക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കും. ദയവായി സംഭാവന നൽകുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 5 പേരുമായി പങ്കിടുക.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-subramanian-ganesan-1

campaigns.title: Help Our Teen Daughter Fight Brain Tumor
campaigns.description: My name is Kabita, I am a house maid, and I am raising funds for my 15-year-old daughter, who lives with us in Hyderabad...

messages.en:
Our friend Kabita's teenage daughter is fighting a brain tumor and needs our help urgently. This brave girl has her whole life ahead of her, but her family is struggling with medical costs.

Only 20 days left to raise ₹5.9 lakh for her treatment. Even ₹100 helps save a young life.

Please donate OR share with 5 people - every action counts.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.hi:
हमारी दोस्त कबिता की किशोर बेटी ब्रेन ट्यूमर से लड़ रही है और उसे हमारी मदद की जरूरत है। इस बहादुर बच्ची का पूरा जीवन आगे है, लेकिन परिवार इलाज की लागत से परेशान है।

उसके इलाज के लिए ₹5.9 लाख जुटाने के लिए केवल 20 दिन बचे हैं। ₹100 भी एक जीवन बचाने में मदद करता है।

कृपया दान करें या 5 लोगों के साथ शेयर करें - हर कदम मायने रखता है।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.kn:
ನಮ್ಮ ಸ್ನೇಹಿತೆ ಕಬಿತಾ ಅವರ ಹದಿಹರೆಯದ ಮಗಳು ಮೆದುಳಿನ ಗೆಡ್ಡೆಯಿಂದ ಹೋರಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾಳೆ ಮತ್ತು ನಮ್ಮ ಸಹಾಯ ಬೇಕಾಗಿದೆ। ಈ ಧೈರ್ಯಶಾಲಿ ಮಗುವಿನ ಮುಂದೆ ಪೂರ್ಣ ಜೀವನವಿದೆ, ಆದರೆ ಕುಟುಂಬ ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸಾ ವೆಚ್ಚದಿಂದ ತೊಂದರೆಯಲ್ಲಿದೆ।

ಅವಳ ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆಗೆ ₹5.9 ಲಕ್ಷ ಸಂಗ್ರಹಿಸಲು ಕೇವಲ 20 ದಿನಗಳು ಉಳಿದಿವೆ। ₹100 ಕೂಡ ಒಂದು ಜೀವ ಉಳಿಸಲು ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡುತ್ತದೆ।

ದಯವಿಟ್ಟು ದೇಣಿಗೆ ನೀಡಿ ಅಥವಾ 5 ಜನರೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹಂಚಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ - ಪ್ರತಿ ಕ್ರಿಯೆ ಮುಖ್ಯ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.ta:
நம் நண்பர் கபிதாவின் பதின்வயது மகள் மூளைக் கட்டியுடன் போராடிக்கொண்டிருக்கிறாள், அவளுக்கு நம் உதவி தேவை. இந்த துணிச்சலான குழந்தையின் முன்னால் முழு வாழ்க்கை இருக்கிறது, ஆனால் குடும்பம் சிகிச்சை செலவில் சிரமப்படுகிறது।

அவளது சிகிச்சைக்கு ₹5.9 லட்சம் சேகரிக்க வெறும் 20 நாட்கள் மட்டுமே உள்ளன. ₹100 கூட ஒரு உயிரைக் காப்பாற்ற உதவும்.

தயவுசெய்து நன்கொடை அளிக்கவும் அல்லது 5 பேருடன் பகிர்ந்துகொள்ளவும் - ஒவ்வொரு செயலும் முக்கியம்.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.te:
మా స్నేహితురాలు కబితా కుమార్తె మెదడు కణితితో పోరాడుతున్నారు, ఆమెకు మన సహాయం అవసరం. ఈ ధైర్యవంతురాలి ముందు పూర్తి జీవితం ఉంది, కానీ కుటుంబం చికిత్సా ఖర్చులతో ఇబ్బంది పడుతోంది।

ఆమె చికిత్సకు ₹5.9 లక్షలు సేకరించడానికి కేవలం 20 దినాలు మాత్రమే మిగిలాయి. ₹100 కూడా ఒక ప్రాణాన్ని కాపాడటానికి సహాయపడుతుంది।

దయచేసి దానం చేయండి లేదా 5 మందితో పంచుకోండి - ప్రతి చర్య ముఖ్యం.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.mr:
आमची मैत्रीण कबिताची किशोरवयीन मुलगी ब्रेन ट्यूमरशी लढत आहे आणि तिला आमच्या मदतीची गरज आहे। या धाडसी मुलीचे संपूर्ण आयुष्य पुढे आहे, पण कुटुंब उपचारांच्या खर्चाने अडचणीत आहे।

तिच्या उपचारासाठी ₹5.9 लाख गोळा करण्यासाठी फक्त 20 दिवस उरले आहेत. ₹100 सुद्धा एक जीव वाचवण्यास मदत करतो।

कृपया दान करा किंवा 5 लोकांसोबत शेअर करा - प्रत्येक कृती महत्त्वाची आहे.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.bn:
আমাদের বন্ধু কবিতার কিশোরী মেয়ে ব্রেইন টিউমারের সাথে লড়াই করছে এবং আমাদের সাহায্য প্রয়োজন। এই সাহসী মেয়েটির সামনে পুরো জীবন পড়ে আছে, কিন্তু পরিবার চিকিৎসার খরচ নিয়ে সমস্যায় আছে।

তার চিকিৎসার জন্য ₹৫.৯ লক্ষ সংগ্রহ করতে মাত্র ২০ দিন বাকি। ₹১০০ও একটি জীবন বাঁচাতে সাহায্য করে।

অনুগ্রহ করে দান করুন অথবা ৫ জনের সাথে শেয়ার করুন - প্রতিটি কাজই গুরুত্বপূর্ণ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.gu:
આપણી મિત્ર કબિતાની કિશોરવયની દીકરી બ્રેઇન ટ્યુમર સાથે લડી રહી છે અને તેને આપણી મદદની જરૂર છે. આ બહાદુર બાળકીનું આખું જીવન આગળ છે, પરંતુ કુટુંબ સારવારના ખર્ચથી પરેશાન છે।

તેની સારવાર માટે ₹5.9 લાખ એકત્ર કરવા માટે માત્ર 20 દિવસ બાકી છે. ₹100 પણ એક જીવન બચાવવામાં મદદ કરે છે.

કૃપા કરીને દાન આપો અથવા 5 લોકો સાથે શેર કરો - દરેક પગલું મહત્વપૂર્ણ છે.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.pa:
ਸਾਡੀ ਮਿੱਤਰ ਕਬਿਤਾ ਦੀ ਕਿਸ਼ੋਰ ਧੀ ਬ੍ਰੇਨ ਟਿਊਮਰ ਨਾਲ ਲੜ ਰਹੀ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਉਸਨੂੰ ਸਾਡੀ ਮਦਦ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। ਇਸ ਬਹਾਦੁਰ ਬੱਚੀ ਦੀ ਪੂਰੀ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਅੱਗੇ ਹੈ, ਪਰ ਪਰਿਵਾਰ ਇਲਾਜ ਦੇ ਖਰਚੇ ਨਾਲ ਪਰੇਸ਼ਾਨ ਹੈ।

ਉਸਦੇ ਇਲਾਜ ਲਈ ₹5.9 ਲੱਖ ਇਕੱਠੇ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ ਸਿਰਫ਼ 20 ਦਿਨ ਬਾਕੀ ਹਨ। ₹100 ਵੀ ਇੱਕ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਬਚਾਉਣ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਦਦ ਕਰਦੇ ਹਨ।

ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਦਾਨ ਦਿਓ ਜਾਂ 5 ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਸਾਂਝਾ ਕਰੋ - ਹਰ ਕਦਮ ਮਹੱਤਵਪੂਰਨ ਹੈ।
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j
messages.ml:
നമ്മുടെ സ്നേഹിതയായ കബിതയുടെ കൗമാരക്കാരിയായ മകൾ ബ്രെയിൻ ട്യൂമറിനെതിരെ പോരാടുകയാണ്, അവൾക്ക് നമ്മുടെ സഹായം ആവശ്യമാണ്. ഈ ധീരയായ കുട്ടിയുടെ മുന്നിൽ മുഴുവൻ ജീവിതമുണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ കുടുംബം ചികിത്സാച്ചെലവിൽ ബുദ്ധിമുട്ടുന്നു.

അവളുടെ ചികിത്സയ്ക്കായി ₹5.9 ലക്ഷം സമാഹരിക്കാൻ വെറും 20 ദിവസമേ ബാക്കിയുള്ളൂ. ₹100 പോലും ഒരു ജീവൻ രക്ഷിക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കും.

ദയവായി സംഭാവന നൽകുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 5 പേരുമായി പങ്കിടുക - ഓരോ പ്രവൃത്തിയും പ്രധാനമാണ്.
https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-child-of-kabita-j

campaigns.title: Help my child fight Ewing Sarcoma and live again
campaigns.description: I am raising funds for my child, a 14-year-old girl from Karamadai, Tamil Nadu, who has been diagnosed with Ewing Sarcom...

messages.en:
A parent's worst nightmare - Somasundram's child is fighting Ewing Sarcoma, a rare cancer that's stealing their childhood. The family needs urgent help for treatment that could save their little one's life. With just 22 days left, they still need ₹13.9 lakh of their ₹14 lakh goal. campaigns.title: Support Quality Education For Children
campaigns.description: Think back to your school days: what did you discover about yourself?A time you felt proud of yourself.A story that stayed with you.A moment that sparked your curiosity.A subject or sport that you loved. These discoveries don’t happen by accident. They happen in schools that make space for curiosity, participation, and exploration.Every child deserves an education that makes room for this discovery.At Adhyayan Foundation, we work with government schools to help create joyful learning environments where children have a voice and can discover what they’re good at.To continue and expand this work in underserved regions of the country, we are raising Rs 50 lakhs. Donate to support discoveries!What your support makes possibleOver the past nine years, we’ve partnered with education systems across Arunachal Pradesh, Delhi, Goa, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Nagaland, and Tripura, reaching more than 10,000 schools and 6,000 village libraries.We support school leaders and teachers to reflect on their strengths, challenges, and areas of growth, and support them in taking practical steps toward school improvement according to their unique contexts. On the way, we co-create avenues for support and problem-solving for teachers and school heads.This leads to improvements in how a child experiences learning in school. Classrooms become more interactive, reading spaces come alive, student attendance improves, the school environment becomes more child-friendly, and the school’s local community becomes more involved.An engaging science lesson enabled by school-to-school resource sharing in Tripura.Students discovering a love for football in our Field Connect program in GoaA moment of discovery in a government school we work with in Arunachal Pradesh.A reading corner in a government school in Kalyan: students are fascinated by the books and regularly browse through them.Students discover career options through exposure visits through our Career Compass program in GoaThis year, we’re celebrating the everyday discoveries that happen in good schools: discoveries of words, ideas, stories, confidence, curiosity, and voice.Your support helps create the conditions that make these discoveries possible, from better teaching practices to stronger school leadership and more engaging learning spaces.The AskWe are raising Rs. 50 lakh by 15th June 2026 to continue and expand our work in underserved regions.Your donation supports our team, training, innovations & pilots, and the essential systems behind our work as part of our working capital reserve, ensuring that our programs remain consistent, responsive, and resilient. This means:Schools receive continuous supportTeachers can keep improving their practiceCommunities can rely on us as long-term partners, not just when funding cycles align.Your contribution would enable consistency and reliability on the ground, helping programs bring lasting impact for the children and those supporting them in the ecosystem.Every child deserves a school where they can explore, participate, and discover.Donate now to support quality education in government schools in India!Learn more about our work on our website and our most recent annual report.

હર બાળકની જેમ તેમને પણ શીખવાની અને વધવાની તક જોઈએ
અમારી પાસે માત્ર 23 દિવસ બાકી છે અને ₹45.8 લાખની જરૂર છે
ફક્ત ₹100 પણ એક બાળકનું ભવિષ્ય ઉજ્જવળ બનાવી શકે છે
કૃપા કરીને દાન આપો અથવા 5 લોકો સાથે શેર કરો 🙏

messages.pa:
ਸਾਡੇ ਕਮਿਊਨਿਟੀ ਦੇ ਬੱਚੇ ਚੰਗੀ ਸਿੱਖਿਆ ਤੋਂ ਬਿਨਾਂ ਸੰਘਰਸ਼ ਕਰ ਰਹੇ ਹਨ 😔
ਹਰ ਬੱਚੇ ਵਾਂਗ ਉਹਨਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਵੀ ਸਿੱਖਣ ਅਤੇ ਵਧਣ ਦਾ ਮੌਕਾ ਚਾਹੀਦਾ ਹੈ
ਸਾਡੇ ਕੋਲ ਸਿਰਫ 23 ਦਿਨ ਬਾਕੀ ਹਨ ਅਤੇ ₹45.8 ਲੱਖ ਚਾਹੀਦੇ ਹਨ
ਸਿਰਫ ਹੀ ₹100 ਵੀ ਕਿਸੇ ਬੱਚੇ ਦਾ ਭਵਿੱਖ ਚਮਕਾ ਸਕਦੇ ਹਨ
ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰਕੇ ਦਾਨ ਕਰੋ ਜਾਂ 5 ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਸਾਂਝਾ ਕਰੋ 🙏

messages.ml:
ഞങ്ങളുടെ കമ്മ്യൂണിറ്റിയിലെ കുട്ടികൾ നല്ല വിദ്യാഭ്യാസമില്ലാതെ പരാധീനരാണ് 😔
ഓരോ കുട്ടിയെയും പോലെ അവർക്കും പഠിക്കാനും വളരാനും അവസരം വേണം
നമുക്ക് വെറും 23 ദിവസം മാത്രമേ ബാക്കിയുള്ളൂ, ഇനിയും ₹45.8 ലക്ഷം വേണം
വെറും ₹100 പോലും ഒരു കുട്ടിയുടെ ഭാവി തെളിച്ചമാക്കാൻ കഴിയും
ദയവായി സംഭാവന നൽകുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 5 പേരുമായി പങ്കുവയ്ക്കുക 🙏

campaigns.title: Support High-Quality Education in Government Schools
campaigns.description:
India is home to 6% of the world’s poor, with 130 million of her children relying on the government education system to escape poverty cycles and gain an opportunity to thrive in life. Yet, the 10.8 million government schools  fail to deliver quality learning as most remain under-budgeted and understaffed. This leaves the enablers of the system without the capacity, mindset, and learning tools to provide high-quality learning, denying India’s children the opportunity to use the power of education to overcome social inequity and impoverishment. 
 Simple Education Foundation (SEF) is on a mission to reinvent India’s government education system by providing high-quality learning to all children to fulfil its vision of empowering children to build a better tomorrow. SEF engages with either one or all key enablers that impact the life and learning of a child - Principals, Educators, and Parents and builds their capacity to be aware, invested, and empowered in taking ownership of their schools and putting it on the path of excellence for every child's holistic development.Directly at the school level, in 2022-23 SEF will work with 11 schools to demonstrate them as Centres of Excellence across Delhi (urban) and Uttrakhand (rural), providing high-quality learning to over 1600 children.At a systems level, we are working with the State Council of Educational Research and Training (SCERT), Delhi on an educator development program, Udaan, which influences 80,000 public educators from 2,500 schools in delivering high-quality learning to 25,00,000 children over 3 years. 
 SEF prepares the enablers of learning to provide high-quality learning by integrating 21st century skills & socio-emotional skills with academic learning, daily. Hence transforming government schools in learning, leadership and culture. Through our programs, our goal is to:Provide Holistic High-Quality LearningStrengthen Principal LeadershipBuild Instructor Teaching CapacityMobilise Community and Parent InvestmentEnsure School Sustainability & Lifelong Excellence
